title: astragalus malacus var. layneae
intro: Astragalus malacus var. layneae, also known as Layne's milkvetch, is a perennial plant species belonging to the legume family and native to the western United States. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its adaptogenic and immunomodulatory properties, which help to promote physical and mental resilience. The medicinal properties of Astragalus malacus var. layneae include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, contributing to various health benefits such as enhanced immune function, reduced fatigue, and improved cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used as a tea, capsule, or tincture to leverage its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
